key_themes: Solid 2025 operational beats, revenue/EBITDA growth, EPS surprises, and modest 2026 EBITDA guidance ($750-775M)
Successful green bond refinancing (extended maturities), maintained $0.10 quarterly dividend (~10% yield), European capacity expansion, and fresh $175M legal win vs. Boston Beer
Persistent high net leverage (~5.3x), tight FCF/dividend coverage concerns, mostly Hold analyst consensus with modest PT upside (~$4.5-5.0), and some recent PT reductions
sources_checked: Seeking Alpha (recent Buy reinstatement and prior notes)
MarketBeat, Yahoo Finance, GuruFocus (analyst ratings, warnings)
Web news on Boston Beer lawsuit victory
X/Twitter keyword and semantic search (limited volume)
summary: AMBP posted strong FY2025 results with 12% revenue growth to $5.5B, 10% Adj. EBITDA growth to $739M (beat), 3% shipment growth especially in North America/energy drinks, and an EPS beat, while maintaining its dividend, guiding modestly higher for 2026, and completing a $1.3B green bond refinancing that pushed out maturities. A very recent jury verdict awarding it roughly $175M against Boston Beer adds a positive catalyst, and a fresh Seeking Alpha Buy rating (Apr 18) highlights execution, sustainability tailwinds, and attractive valuation with ~32% upside and 10% yield. The biggest risk is persistently elevated net leverage around 5.3x with dividends/returns partly debt-supported and limited FCF cushion, keeping most Street ratings at Hold ahead of Q1 results on April 23.

recent_posts.highlight: Reinstates Buy rating citing resilient execution, 4Q EBITDA beat, Q1 guidance ahead of consensus, sustainability tailwinds in metal packaging, undemanding valuation, and ~32% upside plus 10% dividend yield.
Jury rules in Ardagh's favor in contract dispute; Boston Beer faces ~$175M damages for failing to meet minimum aluminum can purchase volumes (2021-2025).
Reports on the Boston Beer vs. Ardagh Metal Packaging high-stakes packaging contract dispute and potential fine.
FY2025 revenue +12%, Adj. EBITDA +10% to $739M (above guidance), profit turnaround, maintained dividend, strong NA volumes, and 2026 guidance issued alongside green bond refinancing update.
